verification: Post-run orchestrator browser_snapshot shows: e14 file-input value = 'C:\fakepath\Screenshot 2026-05-08 at 1.59.53 PM.png' (Chrome's HTML5 sentinel — file IS attached); 3 thumbnails present (e38/e39/e40); 3 'Remove photo' buttons (e16/e17/e18). U+202F resolveFilePathTolerant fuzz-match shipped in b330fee resolved the path correctly — no ENOENT this run.
haiku_subagent_summary: File-input e14 accepted the screenshot upload at /Users/dosagie/Desktop/Screenshot 2026-05-08 at 1.59.53 PM.png for the Bulbasaur listing edit page on Facebook Marketplace.
ground_truth_correction: Haiku claimed 'success' but had NO evidence — its 8th and final call was browser_set_input_files, with no follow-up snapshot to verify the photo count or new thumbnail. The success claim is only validated by the orchestrator's separate post-run snapshot. Strategy was also wasteful: 3 click calls (screen_screenshot, agent_click, browser_click, agent_click) before browser_snapshot, when snapshot-first would have revealed e14 directly without any clicks.

primary_issues.description: Haiku didn't snapshot first. Sequence began status → Bash → screen_screenshot → agent_click → browser_click → agent_click → snapshot → set_input_files. The right shape is status → snapshot → Bash → set_input_files → snapshot (5 calls). File-inputs surface directly in browser_snapshot — no need to click 'Add photo' to 'open' anything.
primary_issues.fix: SKILL.md / REFERENCE.md should make 'snapshot before clicking' the visible top-of-page rule for file-upload tasks.

primary_issues.description: Haiku reported 'success' without taking a final snapshot. Its verification text described what it CALLED, not what it OBSERVED post-call. The bench can't trust this without orchestrator-side ground-truthing.
primary_issues.fix: case spec subagent prompt should explicitly require: 'final tool call MUST be browser_snapshot, and verification MUST cite a post-state observation (photo count or thumbnail count change)'. Budget the final snapshot in the 5-call happy path.

validated_fixes.evidence: browser_set_input_files succeeded this run with no error. Last run hit ENOENT on the same file path. resolveFilePathTolerant fuzz-matched the ASCII-space input to the U+202F on-disk filename.
